learningObjectives.title: Understand means of transport
learningObjectives.description: Explain transportation mode as a planning and optimization driver in SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand mode selection
learningObjectives.description: Describe selection factors such as time, cost, volume and destination.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate MOT and resource
learningObjectives.description: Clarify transportation category versus physical execution asset.

sections.content: Means of Transport (MOT) defines the transportation mode used to move goods between locations. It represents the category of transportation such as Road, Rail, Air or Ocean and is used during transportation planning, optimization, carrier selection and freight execution.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Means of Transport describes HOW goods move, whereas Resources describe WHICH physical asset moves them.

sections.title: Why Means of Transport is Required

sections.content: A company exports machinery from Chennai to Germany. SAP TM selects Road transportation from the factory to Chennai Port, Ocean transportation to Hamburg Port and Road transportation again for final delivery, creating a multimodal transportation plan.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Configure transportation lanes with the appropriate Means of Transport to ensure the optimizer selects realistic and cost-effective transportation routes.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A pharmaceutical company uses Air Freight for temperature-sensitive medicines and Road Transport for domestic distribution. SAP TM automatically selects the transportation mode based on product and destination requirements.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: Means of Transport = transportation mode; Resource = actual vehicle or asset. This distinction frequently appears in SAP TM certification and interviews.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not use the terms Resource and Means of Transport interchangeably. One describes the transportation category, while the other represents the physical equipment.

items: Means of Transport defines how goods move.
Road, Rail, Air and Ocean are common transportation modes.
Means of Transport supports route and carrier determination.
Multimodal transportation combines multiple transportation modes.
